Friday Harbor周五港
№ 005 Wednesday, April 29, 2026 2026年4月29日星期三 Seattle, WA · 47.6° N, 122.3° W 西雅图 · 北纬 47.6°，西经 122.3°

When the Model Draws Seattle 当模型来画西雅图

One line to Claude: 'directly generate City Council in Seattle data, output as GeoJSON.' No list, no example. The model returned 7 district polygons (D1–D7), median 457 m off, worst case D4 at 1.7 km. Where its dividing lines match the city's, you see what it knows. Where they don't, you see exactly what it's guessing. 一句话给 Claude：「直接生成西雅图市议会选区数据，输出为 GeoJSON」。没有清单，没有样例。模型返回了 7 个选区多边形（D1–D7），中位偏移 457 米，最大 D4 偏 1.7 公里。它画对的分界线，是它真知道的；画错的，就是它在猜的。

The notebook札记

The prompt here was a single line. "Directly generate City Council in Seattle data, output as GeoJSON." No list of districts, no example, no map. The model decides on its own how many districts there are, what they're called, where they sit. The result is rendered on the right, against the city's actual published district boundaries on the left, both panels using the same projection and the same Seattle silhouette as a baseline.

The model returned 7 polygons named D1 through D7 — which is correct. Seattle has 7 district seats elected by district plus 2 at-large seats, and the model knew that. Each polygon was drawn with 17 to 26 vertices, attempting to follow what the model believes are the major dividing lines: the I-5 trench separating D6 from D5 in the north and D3 from the rest of the central city, the Ship Canal cutting D6 / D5 / D4 off from D7 / D3 / D2, the Duwamish River separating D1 (West Seattle and South Park) from the rest, MLK Way running down the spine of D2 through Rainier Valley, the Lake Washington shore on the east side of D2, D3, D4.

The misses are uneven. D2 lands within 296 m of its real centroid — the model has the Rainier Valley spine in clear focus. D6 is within 378 m, D1 within 445 m, D7 within 457 m. The biggest misses are D5 (1.25 km) and D4 (1.7 km) — the two most-northern districts, where the dividing lines are subtler and the underlying neighborhoods (Lake City, Northgate, Pinehurst, Wedgwood, View Ridge) generate little internet text the model could anchor against. Median drift across the 7: 457 m. Mean: 733 m.

Visually, the polygons themselves carry the information about where the model is uncertain. D7 is the most over-confident — the model gives it a clean Magnolia peninsula even though it doesn't really know the Discovery Park bluff line. D4 reaches too far east into Lake Washington at Sand Point because the model knows the district includes Sand Point but doesn't know where the Magnuson Park edge actually falls. D5's southern boundary is a straight line where the real district has small staircase notches at every arterial. The city's boundary draftspeople had a survey department and forty years of redistricting practice to lean on. The model has only what has been written about Seattle, and that material almost never describes where one council district ends and the next begins.

这一次的提问只有一句话：「直接生成西雅图市议会选区数据，输出为 GeoJSON。」 没有给选区清单，没有给样例，没有给地图。模型自己决定有几个选区、叫什么、在哪里。 返回的结果显示在右边，左边是市政府公布的真实选区边界。两张图共用同一个投影、同一份西雅图轮廓作底。

模型返回了 7 个多边形，命名 D1 到 D7——这一项是对的。 西雅图市议会确有 7 个选区议席外加 2 个 at-large 议席，模型知道这件事。 每个多边形包含 17 至 26 个顶点，试图沿着模型认为是真实分界的几条线走： 把 D6 与 D5、D3 与中央其他选区分开的 I-5 高速；把 D6 / D5 / D4 与 D7 / D3 / D2 分开的船运河； 把 D1（西雅图西区与 South Park）与其他区域分开的 Duwamish 河； 贯穿 D2 整个 Rainier Valley 的 MLK 大道； D2、D3、D4 东侧的华盛顿湖湖岸。

偏差的分布并不均匀。D2 的中位偏移只有 296 米—— 这一区的 Rainier Valley 主轴在模型眼里很清晰。 D6 偏 378 米，D1 偏 445 米，D7 偏 457 米。 偏得最大的是 D5（1.25 公里）和 D4（1.7 公里）——两个最北端的选区。 这两个区的内部分界更微妙，背后的街区（Lake City、Northgate、Pinehurst、Wedgwood、View Ridge） 在英文互联网上能产出的文本极少，模型基本无锚点可依。 7 个选区整体的中位偏移：457 米，均值 733 米。

视觉上，多边形本身就在告诉你模型在哪里没把握。D7 是最「过度自信」的—— 模型给了它一个干净利落的 Magnolia 半岛，尽管它并不真的知道 Discovery Park 那道悬崖在哪里。 D4 在 Sand Point 处向东深入华盛顿湖太远——模型知道这一区包含 Sand Point， 但不知道 Magnuson 公园那条岸线具体怎么走。 D5 的南界是一条直线，而真实的 D5 南界沿每一条干线都有小阶梯状的凹凸。 市政府的边界绘制者背后有一支测绘队和四十年的重划经验。 模型背后只有「关于西雅图被写下来的那些东西」， 而那些材料里几乎从不告诉你某个市议会选区究竟在哪里结束、下一个在哪里开始。

The provenance出处

  • Real boundaries: City of Seattle Open Data — the 7 City Council districts (D1–D7), shore-clipped layer from the Seattle Tree Canopy 2016/2021 dataset (which co-publishes the same official council district boundaries). Coordinates rounded to 3 decimal places (~110 m precision) for compactness; geometry preserved.
  • AI boundaries: single line issued to Claude Opus 4.7 within this writing session — "directly generate City Council in Seattle data, output as GeoJSON." No external lookups, no list of districts, no example. The returned polygons are stored verbatim — not reshaped, not snapped, not cleaned. The model's output is the data. File: seattle-council-claude.geojson.
  • Both layers projected with d3-geo's Mercator with manual scale + translate, centred on Seattle's bbox [-122.46, 47.49] → [-122.23, 47.74]. (Albers' conic distortion warps at city scale.)
  • Centroid drift figure computed locally as the Haversine distance between the centroid of each Claude polygon (D1..D7) and the centroid of the matching real polygon by district number; statistics across the 7 pairs.
  • Source map & build script: src/pages/friday-harbor/2026-04-29-when-the-model-draws-seattle.astro; data files: public/data/seattle-council-real.geojson and seattle-council-claude.geojson.
  • 真实边界：西雅图市政府开放数据——7 个市议会选区（D1–D7），沿海岸裁切的图层（来自市府的 Seattle Tree Canopy 2016/2021 数据集，附带的边界图层与官方选区一致）。坐标保留 3 位小数（约 110 米精度）以减小文件体积；几何形状保留完整。
  • AI 边界：在本次写作会话中，向 Claude Opus 4.7 发送一句提问——「直接生成西雅图市议会选区数据，输出为 GeoJSON。」不允许查阅外部资料，不给选区清单，不给样例。返回的多边形原样保存——未做整形、未对齐网格、未清洗。模型输出本身就是数据。文件：seattle-council-claude.geojson。
  • 两层数据均用 d3-geo 的墨卡托投影 + 手动 scale/translate 渲染，中心定在西雅图的 bbox [-122.46, 47.49] → [-122.23, 47.74]。（Albers 圆锥投影在城市尺度下会扭曲。）
  • 质心偏移：本地计算，每个 D1..D7 选区的 Claude 多边形质心 vs 同号实际选区多边形质心的 Haversine 距离，对 7 对取统计量。
  • 源码与构建脚本：src/pages/friday-harbor/2026-04-29-when-the-model-draws-seattle.astro；数据文件：public/data/seattle-council-real.geojson 与 seattle-council-claude.geojson。

University of Washington.

华盛顿大学。
